topic_title: Deterministic systems — same input always gives the same output
verdict: PASS — all 10 checks green. Reading meets the gold bar set by 2-1-what-makes-a-good-specification.reading.md: matching six-section structure, identical beginner tone, equivalent formatting density (table, bold-term definitions, numbered Worked Example steps). Word count 1391 is within the standard-profile window [1200–1600]. Primary source [1] cited throughout. No frontmatter, no assessment references, no scope creep, no forward teaching. Diagram embedded correctly inside Key Concepts. All 10 sampled faithfulness assertions trace cleanly to corpus.

format_discipline.notes: H1 is the first non-empty line. All six required sections present in the correct order. No extra or renamed headings.

length.notes: 1391 words at 200 wpm = 6.96 min, within the 6–8 min standard-profile window.

citations.coverage_estimate: high — the majority of factual claims carry inline citations across all sections

no_assessment_references.notes: No assessment IDs, due weeks, grade weights, or portfolio references found anywhere in the reading.

diagram_in_key_concepts.location: Key Concepts section, under subsection 'The diagram below shows the core idea' (reading.md lines 64–67)
diagram_in_key_concepts.notes: Diagram is embedded inline within ## Key Concepts, satisfying the requirement. Caption is present.

mention_and_defer_usage.form: bare name + topic pointer ('covered in topic 1.3')

scope.notes: All out-of-scope concepts (non-deterministic, probabilistic, algorithm) appear only as bare names with a single deferred pointer. No teaching or definition of those terms. Scope is cleanly bounded to the topic headline.

beginner_readability.notes: All introduced terms (deterministic system, predictability, reproducibility, hidden inputs) are bolded and plain-language defined at first appearance. Concepts are introduced concrete-first throughout (table of examples precedes formal properties; navigation app example precedes hidden inputs list). Sentences are short and active. Zero hedging phrases found.

formatting.notes: All paragraphs are within the ~4–6 sentence limit. The Worked Example is a numbered sequence (Step 1 / Step 2 / Step 3). Inline examples are present in Key Concepts (4-row table, navigation app). Bold-term / plain-meaning definition shape used for all introduced terms.

samples.assertion: A deterministic system's output is completely fixed by its input
Four-row examples table (calculator, sort, traffic light, currency converter)
Predictability = reason about output before running
Reproducibility = same output on different machines, times, places
Navigation app: time of day is a hidden input changing the route
Vending machine worked example (£1.20, B3, £0.10 returned)
In Practice bullets: financial, database, build tools

gold_bar_assessment.notes: Structure matches: H1 title, six sections in order, subsections within Key Concepts, numbered steps in Worked Example. Beginner tone is equivalent — direct, second-person, no assumed knowledge. Formatting density is equivalent: bold-term definitions, table in Key Concepts, numbered worked steps, bullet lists with bold lead phrases in Key Takeaways. The reading is a credible peer of the gold example.
